rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RODNEY BRUCE MIDDLETON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/8/23. No earlier availability. Examiner unable to complete thorough review. The verification of the second amended petition refers to the amended petition. Prior attorney's invoice 24 dated 1/21/20 still does not have adequate descriptions of what was filed or delivered, etc., on 6/15/18, 7/2/18, 8/1/18, 9/4/18, 12/7/18, 6/25/19, and 12/30/19, and the e-filing fee should be $5, not $11.50. The filing fee for the petition to approve the creditor's claim was $435, not $458. The filing fee for this petition was $435, not $450. (The prayer refers to the incorrect exhibits, but this will be overlooked in order for the issues to be kept to a minimum.) Please do NOT file a third amended pleading, which requires a full review of the entire pleading. If amending any portion of this pleading, please file just an amendment to the second amended petition. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/25/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILMA COREA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Despite the delays in getting a petition filed at all and then these continued hearings for the entirely inadequate accounting petition that was filed seven months after the estate should have closed by law, issues still have not been addressed: still no proposed order and no courtesy copies have been delivered to this examiner, and no bank statements have been filed. The other issues posted in the tentative ruling for the 8/31/22 hearing, regarding the incomplete accounting spanning more than two years, also have not yet been addressed. The Court to determine if the executor should be suspended for failing to administer the estate or if other penalties are warranted (e.g., removal after citation or surcharge of statutory compensation or sanctions). [Probate Code Sections 8500 and 12205.] (If the executor is suspended, notice must be given to the surety who has filed a court bond. [Probate Code Section 1213(b).]) Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAUREEN E. CROWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/8/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. [Probate Code Section 12201.] The attorney for the executor was the attorney for the petitioners. This appears to be a conflict. A petition requesting additional time for estate administration and reporting on the status of the estate must set forth the condition of the estate and an estimate of the time needed to close administration of the estate. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/30/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MICHAEL J. HALLORAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/8/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proof of service of notice has been filed. The accounting is entirely inadequate: no Summary of Account; no detailed schedules; and no original bank statements. [Probate Code Sections 2620(a) and 2620(c)(2) and CRC 7.575.] The Court Investigator reported in April 2021 that there was about $2,000 in a checking account and that rent was $258/month. There was reportedly also a special needs trust created by the conservatee's parents. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/25/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ATALIE XIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No inventory and appraisal has been filed with a proof of mailing thereof, despite the following reminders e-mailed to both counsel on 10/03/2022: 1) A separate final inventory and appraisal must be filed for each minor's estate before the next calling, regardless of the status of the probate proceeding in Alameda County. The minor's right to receive distribution must be inventoried as such as a single item, with or without assigning any value. If no specific value can be assigned, a statement such as "not appraised at this time" or "to be appraised upon final distribution" should be made by the referee on the inventory and appraisal form. 2) The life insurance benefits must be inventoried and appraised. If not payable in a lump sum, the benefits must be appraised by the referee. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PRECIOCISIMA B PRIMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete legal description, street address, and assessors parcel number of real property, the complete description of each individual security, and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] The I&A listed 36, not 38 bonds, all of which must be listed in detail if they have not been redeemed. The prayer requests that the real property be distributed to the two sons "in equal shares", but the proposed order shows as "joint tenants". Did the other beneficiary consent to holding the real property as a joint tenant? A supplement to the petition must be filed, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the executor, to address these issues. Courtesy copies of the supplement and any other papers filed before the 1/18/23 hearing must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/9/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA HOLLANDER LYON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 6/23/03. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 12/18/23. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 12/18/23, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DIANE RAIKE MITAU TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 8,2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Termination Of Trust And Distribution Of Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests termination of the trust, alleging that she holds a presently exercisable general power of appointment. Court to determine whether a power of appointment that can only take effect after the death of the settlor is a "presently" exercisable power of appointment. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRY VARELA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for counsel to update the court if two heirs' whereabouts have been known as of the hearing. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEVEN MAURICE GRAINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continue to 1/18/2023. The following issues to be address at least 7 days prior: (1) Petition to seek the appointment of Adminsitrator with Will Annexed as the nominee was not named by the will to act. Prob. Code Sec. 8440 (2) allegation of bond as the will does not waive bond as to peititoner; (3) notice to include remote appearance. LRSF 4.1.D.(6). Examiners continuance are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW HAMILTON BURKE TONKEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/30/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A supplement to the petition must be filed, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, to address the following issues: (1) How old are the minors? (2) Who are the persons having legal custody of the minors with whom the minors reside? (3) When did the decedent's parents die? [LRSF 14.18 D.] (4) The petitioner and alternate executors are missing from item 8 (or Attachment 8) to the petition. Proof of service of notice given directly to the minors (as well as to the person(s) having legal custody of the minors with whom the minors reside) must also be filed. Courtesy copies of supplement and proof of service must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/20/23. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] They should not be redacted. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
